title: Kubernetes Purpose, Architecture, Reconciliation, and Environment Evidence · Kubernetes Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release review compares fresh kubernetes purpose, architecture, reconciliation, and environment evidence choices and accepts only answers tied to observable API, controller, workload, network, storage, or policy evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for cluster architecture and reconciliation evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace API server, etcd, scheduler, controller manager, kubelet, container runtime, network proxy, DNS, and add-on responsibilities for one workload transition.
Explain how API requests, admission, persisted desired state, watches, caches, and component status relate without treating any one cache as authority.
Trace desired state, observed state, controller decisions, idempotent actions, status updates, and eventual convergence through a changed failure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz K8s Control Plane Node Components
steps.instruction:
Order the K8s Control Plane Node Components orchestration evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz K8s Control Plane Node Components.
steps.why: Correct order keeps K8s Control Plane Node Components assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz K8s Control Plane Node Components, keep K8s Control Plane Node Components connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A quiz team sees kubectl 1.36.2, an unfamiliar current context, a healthy API endpoint, and a Deployment whose desired replicas differ from observed status. Trace client, API server, admission, storage, controller, scheduler, kubelet, and runtime authority without treating reconciliation as application correctness. Competency probe: Trace API server, etcd, scheduler, controller manager, kubelet, container runtime, network proxy, DNS, and add-on responsibilities for one workload transition.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so K8s Control Plane Node Components has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz K8s Control Plane Node Components, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Control Plane Node Components precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz K8s Control Plane Node Components, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A quiz team sees kubectl 1.36.2, an unfamiliar current context, a healthy API endpoint, and a Deployment whose desired replicas differ from observed status. Trace client, API server, admission, storage, controller, scheduler, kubelet, and runtime authority without treating reconciliation as application correctness. Competency probe: Trace API server, etcd, scheduler, controller manager, kubelet, container runtime, network proxy, DNS, and add-on responsibilities for one workload transition.

For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz K8s Control Plane Node Components, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: The scheduler starts containers and continuously repairs every unhealthy application.

options.text: Capture client and server versions, context, namespace, API discovery, current object state, ownership, and immutable release identity for the quiz K8s Control Plane Node Components case
Predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, security, disruption, and cleanup effects for the quiz K8s Control Plane Node Components case
Review the smallest manifest, Kustomize, Helm, policy, or runbook increment in the deterministic browser simulator for the quiz K8s Control Plane Node Components case
Reject one failure, record repair and rollback, then name authorized disposable-cluster transfer evidence for the quiz K8s Control Plane Node Components case
